text:
Yes my understanding is that is was Ontology based. Back in 2021 or so I proposed to my boss the idea of combining LLMs inspired by GPT with the traditional symbolic methods of AI to build a new modern incarnation of the old expert systems. I was just a research assistant at the time and he had many other commitments so the conversation never went anywhere.

On the subject of ontologies, my friend Gerry Gleason from Chicago studied under the former Chilean Minister of Treasury in the Allende administration before Pinochet's coup. He'd been the one to spearhead the Project Cybercyn distributed computer management system.
<https://gerry.decko.org/Permanent_Domains_of_Human_Concern>

<https://tinyurl.com/yjd3uw2r>

I'm looking to use an existing economic ontology called Valueflows to develop a distributed value production, exchange, and coordination platform to help my people develop and thrive economically when we have traditionally been immiserated and impoverished by colonization. I'm looking to combine it with a knowledge management app called Anytype and local AI orchestration to enable the creation of autonomous economic agents which aid humans in the loop with economic management and coordination tasks.

<https://www.valueflo.ws/>

attachments.fallback: The MIT Press Reader: Project Cybersyn: Chile's Radical Experiment in Cybernetic Socialism
attachments.text: Fifty years ago, a military coup violently ended Chile’s political experiment with socialism, and with it the nation’s technological experiment with cybernetic management.
attachments.title: Project Cybersyn: Chile's Radical Experiment in Cybernetic Socialism

text: Oh man, that 70’s style. I’ll have to check this out.
